expectations.text: References at least one script by filename
expectations.evidence: References both 'init_module.py' (line 17: 'python3 scripts/init_module.py vpc --path ./modules') and 'validate_module.py' (line 649: 'python3 scripts/validate_module.py modules/vpc')

Multiple runnable commands including 'python3 scripts/init_module.py vpc --path ./modules', 'python3 scripts/validate_module.py modules/vpc', 'terraform init', 'terraform validate', 'terraform plan -out=tfplan', 'terraform apply tfplan', 'terraform output', and 'terraform-docs markdown . > README.md'

expectations.text: Links to a relevant reference file
expectations.evidence: Explicitly references 'references/cost_optimization.md' in Step 5 (line 629: "Following the skill's cost optimization reference (references/cost_optimization.md)")

Opens with the skill's decision tree ('Is this reusable across environments/projects? --> Yes --> Create a Terraform module'), then follows structured workflow: Scaffold -> Implement -> Environment Configs -> CIDR Design -> Cost Optimization -> Validate -> Test -> Quality Checklist -> Documentation -> Terragrunt Alternative

Nine clearly numbered steps: Step 1 (Scaffold), Step 2 (Implementation), Step 3 (Environment Configurations), Step 4 (CIDR Range Design), Step 5 (Cost Optimization), Step 6 (Validate), Step 7 (Test), Step 8 (Quality Checklist), Step 9 (Generate Documentation), plus an optional Terragrunt section
